entry.title: 'Ghostcommit' hides prompt injection in images to fool AI agents, steal secrets
entry.summary: A PNG hiding a prompt injection could steal your repo's secrets, researchers demonstrate. The technique, dubbed 'Ghostcommit,' slipped past AI code reviewers CodeRabbit and Bugbot, which never open image files at all, then convinced a coding agent to read a repo's .env and write every secret into the code as a list of

entry.title: The new GPT-5.6 family: Luna, Terra, Sol
entry.summary: OpenAI's latest flagship model hit general availability this morning , and comes in three sizes: Luna, Terra, and Sol (from smallest to largest). The new models are priced per 1M input/output tokens as Luna $1/$6, Terra $2.50/$15, Sol $5/$30. For comparison, the Claude Opus series are $5/$25 and the Claude Fable 5 is $

entry.title: not much happened today
entry.summary: **OpenAI** rolled out **GPT-5.6** featuring a new model stratification with tiers **Luna / Terra / Sol** and effort levels including **Max** and **Ultra**, introducing complex configuration options. The launch faced UX challenges with the **ChatGPT Work / Codex** split, prompting rapid corrective actions including usag

entry.title: SK Hynix’s US shares jump 13% on Nasdaq debut
entry.summary: South Korean memory chipmaker’s US-listed stock price rose as high as $177 after pricing at $149

entry.title: ollama v0.32.0 released
entry.summary: What's Changed create: select the qwen3.5 parser and renderer for Qwen3.5/Next by @jessegross in #17078 launch: warn before old agent models by @ParthSareen in #17063 cmd: agent UI by @ParthSareen in #17017 Full Changelog : v0.31.2...v0.32.0-rc0

entry.title: NYC-based Vendelux, a live B2B event information platform for CMOs and marketing and sales teams, raised a $50M Series B led by Tribeca Venture Partners (Lucia Moses/Business Insider)
entry.summary: Lucia Moses / Business Insider : NYC-based Vendelux, a live B2B event information platform for CMOs and marketing and sales teams, raised a $50M Series B led by Tribeca Venture Partners — - Vendelux, a company that helps CMOs figure out their live events strategy, raised $50 million. — It plans to use the new round …

entry.title: llama.cpp b9976 released
entry.summary: Fix conditional to display 'LLAMA_SPLIT_MODE_TENSOR not implemented f…

entry.title: Korea’s World-Beating Stocks Are Now Trading Cheaper Than Ever
entry.summary: South Korea’s blistering stock rally has left investors facing an unlikely backdrop: equities trading at record-low valuations.

entry.title: transformers v5.13.1 released
entry.summary: Patch release v5.13.1 This patch is focused on enabling transformers for the latest release of vllm! Be more defensive with remap_legacy_layer_types for custom models ( #47245 ) from @hmellor Fix custom code which doesn't know about the new linear layer type names ( #47174 ) from @hmellor Fix case where _LazyAutoMappin

entry.title: AMD RX 9070 GRE collapses to $499 to save 1440p gaming — RDNA 4 price slips 9% to steal a piece of Nvidia's mid-range pie
entry.summary: AMD's Radeon RX 9070 GRE has received its first price cut since launching outside China, making the 1440p-focused RDNA 4 graphics card a more compelling alternative to Nvidia's RTX 5060 Ti 16GB.

entry.title: How to Evaluate General-Purpose Robot Policies for Real-World Deployment
entry.summary: Robotics foundation models have made remarkable progress. Today's best systems can follow natural language instructions to pick, place, sort, and manipulate a...

entry.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
entry.summary: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out again and again: compa

entry.title: Current AI market dynamics point to frontier models becoming commodity infrastructure as the token crunch eases, with value shifting to products built on top (Benedict Evans)
entry.summary: Benedict Evans : Current AI market dynamics point to frontier models becoming commodity infrastructure as the token crunch eases, with value shifting to products built on top — There are only two things you can say with certainty about token prices: we're in a supply crunch, and this is unstable.

entry.title: Australia warns of global campaign targeting vulnerable CMS platforms
entry.summary: The Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC) issued an alert about a global exploitation campaign targeting vulnerable content management systems (CMS) and plugins. [...]

entry.title: sqlite-utils 4.1
entry.summary: Release: sqlite-utils 4.1 The first dot-release since 4.0 a few days ago , introducing a number of minor new features. sqlite-utils insert and sqlite-utils upsert now accept a --code option for providing a block of Python code (or a path to a .py file) that defines a rows() function or rows iterable of rows to insert,

entry.title: Held Prize call for nominations (+ call for postdocs)
entry.summary: Here at the National Academy of Sciences, it seems that my first job is to serve on the selection committee for the prestigious Michael and Sheila Held Prize in combinatorial and discrete optimization and related areas. The committee chair, my former MIT colleague Madhu Sudan (now at Harvard), invited me to share the f

entry.title: OpenAI admits it "didn't get everything quite right" with ChatGPT Work launch and scrambles to fix UX and costs
entry.summary: Following the launch of ChatGPT Work and GPT-5.6 Sol, OpenAI has acknowledged significant issues: excessive compute usage, a confusing transition to the desktop interface for chats and projects, an unclear distinction between Codex and ChatGPT Work, and regressions in existing workflows. In some cases, GPT-5.6 Sol repo

entry.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra reportedly solves a 50-year-old math problem in under an hour
entry.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra produced a proof of the Cycle Double Cover Conjecture in under an hour, using 64 subagents working in parallel. The conjecture had remained unsolved for 50 years. Mathematician Thomas Bloom calls the proof surprisingly elementary but criticizes the lack of citations for known prior work. The

entry.title: A look at the growing anti-AI movement in the Bay Area, as the disappearance of Sam Kirchner, co-founder of a hard-line activist group, has the movement on edge (Zusha Elinson/Wall Street Journal)
entry.summary: Zusha Elinson / Wall Street Journal : A look at the growing anti-AI movement in the Bay Area, as the disappearance of Sam Kirchner, co-founder of a hard-line activist group, has the movement on edge — The resistance to artificial intelligence is growing over fears about human extinction—but one activist's disappearance

entry.title: AirDNA: during the FIFA World Cup group stage, platforms such as Airbnb saw 52K+ new listings in US host cities, while hotel bookings fell short of expectations (Financial Times)
entry.summary: Financial Times : AirDNA: during the FIFA World Cup group stage, platforms such as Airbnb saw 52K+ new listings in US host cities, while hotel bookings fell short of expectations — Hotel bookings fall short of expectations as price-conscious fans opt to stay in American homes

entry.title: vllm v0.25.0 released
entry.summary: vLLM v0.25.0 Release Notes Highlights This release features 558 commits from 232 contributors (64 new)! Model Runner V2 is now the default for all dense models ( #44443 ). Building on quantized-model support from the previous release, MRv2 is now the standard execution path, with new support for EVS ( #46535 ), realtim

entry.title: sglang v0.5.15 released
entry.summary: Highlights GLM-5.2 NVFP4, tuned for production : We took time this cycle to tune GLM-5.2 NVFP4 on Blackwell for optimized production serving. It now runs at 500+ tok/s/user on 8x B300, 450 on 4x GB300 (bs=1). Run GLM-5.2 with our cookbook . Spec V2 by default : zero-overhead scheduling via CUDA-graphable DSA draft-exte

entry.title: Desktop Canary v2.2.11-canary.7

Commit Information Based on changes since v2.2.11-canary.6 Commit count: 27 9b41a57fd2 ♻️ refactor: move LLM finalization into agent runtime ( #17078 ) (Arvin Xu) cc9927e134 🚸 feat(desktop): gate the in-app Browser tab behind a Labs toggle ( — Also covered by: lobehub/lobe-chat, lobehub/lobe-chat

entry.title: Banks’ Record Bond Short Sparks Quest for Answers
entry.summary: For the first time in recent history, primary dealers have gone net short on debt they used to hold billions of dollars of.

entry.title: Warsh and US Inflation Will Set Tone for July Fed Decision
entry.summary: Kevin Warsh is about to make his first appearance before Congress as Federal Reserve chairman, and during two days of testimony he’ll have new US inflation data to parse with lawmakers.

entry.title: Ollama: all aboard open models
entry.summary: Serving 8.9 million developers, Ollama has raised $88M from Benchmark, Theory Ventures, 8VC, Y Combinator, and many incredible angel investors.

entry.title: claude-code v2.1.207 released
entry.summary: What's changed Auto mode is now available without CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE opt-in on Bedrock, Vertex AI, and Foundry; disable via disableAutoMode in settings Fixed the terminal freezing and keystrokes lagging while streaming responses containing very long lists, tables, paragraphs, or code blocks Fixed remote manag

entry.title: Why Fox is ‘the most Disciplined’ Media Entertainment Company in the Streaming Era
entry.summary: Media veteran Michael Wolf discusses Allen & Company’s annual Sun Valley Conference and breaks down the mega deals that are reshaping the entertainment industry. He explains why free ad-supported content is the unsung winner of the streaming wars, and digs into the ways AI is changing the value of content. (Source: Blo

entry.title: Hotspot temperature sensor on Nvidia's Blackwell gaming GPUs is still accessible if you have access to Nvidia's internal MODS tool — Nvidia RTX 5070 Ti caught throttling at 107°C over poor TIM application
entry.summary: Nvidia decided to hide the hotspot temperature on its RTX 50 series, but internal diagnostic tools, such as Nvidia's own "MODS," can still read it. The resulting data reveals how some GPUs can overheat and throttle easily, which could be why the sensor was kept hidden in the first place.
